METHOD ptolemy.actor.gui Class CompositeActorSimpleApplication java.lang.Object ptolemy.actor.gui.CompositeActorSimpleApplication public class CompositeActorSimpleApplication extends java.lang.Object This application creates one or more Ptolemy II mo

This application creates one or more Ptolemy II models given a classname on the command line, and then executes those models, each in its own thread. Each specified class should be derived from CompositeActor, and should have a constructor

CompositeActor, and should have a constructor that takes a single argument, an instance of Workspace. If the model does not contain a manager, then one will be created for it. The model is not displayed, models that have actors that ext

not displayed, models that have actors that extend Placeable should instead use CompositeActorApplication . The command-line arguments can also set parameter values for any parameter in the models, with the name given relative to the top-level

ls, with the name given relative to the top-level entity. For example, to specify the iteration count in an SDF model, you can invoke this on the command line as follows: CLASSPATH=$PTII export CLASSPATH java ptolemy.actor.gui.CompositeA

ort CLASSPATH java ptolemy.actor.gui.CompositeActorSimpleApplication \ -director.iterations 1000 \ -class ptolemy.domains.sdf.demo.Butterfly.Butterfly This assumes that the model given by the specified class name has a director named "dir

specified class name has a director named "director" with a parameter named "iterations". If more than one model is given on the command line, then the parameter values will be set for all models that have such a parameter. Since: Ptolemy

. startRun public void startRun ( CompositeActor model) throws IllegalActionException If the specified model has a manager and is not already running, then execute the model in a new thread. Otherwise, do nothing. If the model cont

hread. Otherwise, do nothing. If the model contains an atomic entity that implements Placeable, we create create an instance of ModelFrame, if nothing implements Placeable, then we do not create an instance of ModelFrame. This allows us to

e an instance of ModelFrame. This allows us to run non-graphical models on systems that do not have a display. We then start the model running. Parameters: model - The model to execute. Throws: IllegalActionException See Also: Manager.startRun()

ean _parseArg (java.lang.String arg) throws java.lang.Exception Parse a command-line argument. The recognized arguments, which result in this method returning true, are summarized below: If the argument is "-class", then attem

zed below: If the argument is "-class", then attempt to interpret the next argument as the fully qualified classname of a class to instantiate as a ptolemy model. The model will be created, added to the directory of models, and then executed.

ed to the directory of models, and then executed. If the argument is "-help", then print a help message. If the argument is "-test", then set a flag that will abort execution of any created models after two seconds. If the argument is "-version", t

fter two seconds. If the argument is "-version", then print a short version message. If the argument is "", then ignore it. Otherwise, the argument is ignored and false is returned. Returns: True if the argument is understood, false otherwise. Throws
